title: Stevens-Johnson syndrome - NHS
text:
ikely to get it again if you take that medicine again or other similar medicines you have a weakened immune system – for example, from having a condition like HIV or AIDS , or treatments like chemotherapy a close family member has had Stevens-Johnson syndrome – sometimes your genes can mean you're at higher risk of getting it 
Important
Stevens-Johnson syndrome is rare and the risk of getting it is low, even if you're taking a medicine that can cause it.
Symptoms of Stevens-Johnson syndrome 
Stevens-Johnson syndrome can start with flu-like symptoms, such as a high temperature, sore throat, cough and joint pain.
A rash appears a few days later. It's made up of circular patches that are darker in the middle and lighter around the outside.
